mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week as earnings expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST move in opposite directions, or interaction term IC is negative over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates positive equity returns over 20 days specifically during post-recession recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery periods, or the mechanism reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP confirms underlying economic expansion, providing a persistent positive baseline for S&P 500 returns over the following week as corporate revenue forecasts are revised upward.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation exceeds 3.5 percent annually, markets anticipate aggressive monetary tightening that compresses valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5%, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days through improved consumer spending outlooks.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields negative or flat 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered cases, or the interaction effect vanishes in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp year-over-year contractions in housing starts signal a collapsing construction sector and reduced household wealth, driving negative S&P 500 returns within one week during late-cycle downturns.
failure_condition: HOUST declines > 10% YoY fail to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal produces positive returns in > 55% of observed instances.
